title: Leaf Protein Extraction for Immunoblot (Soybean, Cowpea, Tobacco)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"36eqn","text":"This protein extraction protocol was developed for analysis of protein abundance in leaf tissue by immunoblot. It was optimized for dicot species including Glycine max.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c687k","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"93rc","text":"Quantities - 450 µL protein extraction buffer (PEB) per three 13.4 mm diameter leaf disks (size #7 Humboldt Cork Borer)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fdd4c","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bprfj","text":"Note from Agrisera: Keeping sample volumes in a range of 0.2-0.5 mL has been found to contribute to better extraction results, an upscale in volume is not recommended, if no cork borer available the suggestion is 500 µL PEB per 100 mg of plant tissue. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"coksr","text":"- Final volume ~400 µL ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"20np2","text":"- Expected yield: 1.5 - 6 µg / µL ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2b9lm","text":"- Total yield: 6 - 24 mg protein ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cirub","text":"- Amount of total soluble protein to load per lane 30 µg  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ahlu9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ec2ug","text":"Note: when using the TissueLyser II it is recommended to use 2 mL centrifuge tubes in conjunction with 4mm beads. 1.5mL tubes are narrow at the end and samples will not grind properly if you use the 4mm beads.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

2150)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":8,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":43,"length":10}],"data":{}},{"key":"ag40m","text":"Humboldt brass cork borer set (07-865-10B; Fisher Scientific)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7fp36","text":"13.4 mm diameter, flash-frozen leaf disks","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cfj8i","text":"4x Protein Extraction Buffer (PEB) (8 % SDS (w/v); 40 % glycerol (v/v); 0.25 M Tris HCl (pH 6.8))","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"838v","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"dsmcc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"CBE52C1438C611EBA61A0A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://www.qiagen.com/us/products/human-id-and-forensics/automation/tissuelyser-ii/#orderinginformation"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"CBE52C7B38C611EBA61A0A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://www.fishersci.co.uk/shop/products/spex-sampleprep-stainless-steel-grinding-balls-4mm-diameter/12145950"}},"2":{"type":"notes","mutability":"","data":{"blocks":[{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[{"length":13,"offset":0,"style":"bold"}],"key":"a34ha","text":"(from Abcam) SDS grade is important for high-quality protein separation: a protein stained background along individual gel tracts with indistinct or slightly distinct protein bands are indicative of old or poor quality SDS. This buffer is essentially the same as Laemmli buffer so proteins can be directly loaded onto PAGE gels.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}}}}
steps: Incubate 4x PEB (if stored at 4 o C) at 50 °C for 00:20:00 to re-suspended precipitated SDS in buffer.
Make 500 µL 1x PEB per sample by diluting 4x stock with dH 2 O. It is advised to make more 1x PEB than necessary to avoid running out.
Pre-cool 4mm SPEX stainless steel grinding beads at -80 °C for 00:30:00
Pre-cool the TissueLyser inserts for LN 2 for 00:30:00
Prepare sample tubes for tissue lysis
Using forceps cooled in LN 2 , add one SPEX bead per sample tube.
Insert tubes into pre-cooled TissueLyser II cassettes, ensuring a balanced number of samples between cassettes.

Grind tissue for 00:01:30 at 20 Hz Warning: Do not exceed this frequency, higher frequencies increase the number of cases where the steel beads will break the lid of centrifuge tubes resulting in sample loss. If using the SPEX 2150 beads it is necessary to use 2 mL centrifuge tubes to ensure proper grinding, in 1.5 mL tubes the bead will not reach the tapered bottom leaving samples unground TissueLyser II (Bead Mill, QIAGEN, 85300, https://www.qiagen.com/us/products/human-id-and-forensics/automation/tissuelyser-ii/#orderinginformation )
Remove cassettes from TissueLyser II and submerge them in LN 2 to prevent thawing

Grind tissue for 00:01:30 at 20 Hz This repeat is to ensure all tissue is correctly grounded. There will be odd instances where leaf tissue has not properly ground for reasons such as samples sticking to the edge of tubes, check for this on removal, it may be necessary to repeat again.

Remove tubes with forceps cooled in LN 2 . Samples can be stored at -80 °C for several months before processing further. It is advised not to proceed directly from samples in LN 2 to the next step as they take longer to thaw and resuspend in extraction buffer. A period of 30 mins on dry ice can be beneficial.
Add 450 µL 1x PEB to ground powder (3x 1cm leaf disk).
Vortex immediately (maximum speed) to mix. Approximately 00:01:00 No lumps should be visible at this stage if the sample is ground well.

Heat samples at 95 °C for 00:05:00 to denature proteins and inactivate proteases. Prolonged heating can cause cleavage of peptide bonds leading to artifacts. Do not delay heating after sample buffer addition as not all proteases are denatured by SDS and partially denatured peptides are sensitive to protease degradation.
Vortex heated samples to shear nucleic acids ( 00:00:05 ; max speed)

Spin samples for 00:03:00 at 10000 x g, Room temperature, to pellet insoluble material the pellet should be white/light-grey an intense green color of the pellet can indicate that disruption was not optimal and extraction conditions need to be adjusted (e.g. improved grinding, or adjusting buffer volume)
Transfer supernatant to a new 1.5 mL centrifuge tube and transfer to ice or store at -20 °C for up to a month Be careful not carry over debris
creator: Steven J Burgess
